text.title: Voice Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every turn-based voice agent must enforce: latency budget, VAD recording, short replies, history cap, explicit language, streaming TTS.

rule.statement: Each turn budgets components: VAD ≤100ms, STT ≤300ms, LLM ≤500ms, TTS ≤200ms. Total end-to-end target ≤1000ms; hard ceiling 1500ms before user-perception failure.

rationale.source: Voice UX research (Google, Apple, Amazon human-factors studies)

rule.rationale: Above ~1.5s users perceive the agent as broken / unresponsive. Each component budget is independently optimizable but the total budget is the real contract.

rule.statement: Use VAD-based recording (webrtcvad aggressiveness=2 or Silero) that stops on `silence_ms` of consecutive silence. Forbid fixed-duration `sd.rec(seconds)` in any production deployment.

rationale.source: Variable-utterance traces: 0.5s requests vs 8s requests under one prompt

rule.rationale: Fixed recording truncates long utterances and records silence for short ones; VAD adapts to the actual speech duration and saves STT cost on silence.

rule.statement: Cap `max_response_tokens` at 150-200 (~50 spoken words). Use `tts-1` (not `tts-1-hd`) in realtime. Strip Markdown symbols (`re.sub(r'[*_`#>]', '', text)`) before passing to TTS.

rationale.source: TTS comprehension studies + Markdown-in-audio readability traces

rule.rationale: Long replies feel unnatural and cannot be interrupted; tts-1-hd doubles latency; raw Markdown is read literally ("asterisk asterisk bold").

rule.statement: Truncate `conversation_history` to system prompt + last 10 user/assistant pairs. Older context goes into a summary that lives in the system prompt.

rationale.source: GPT-4o context overflow at ~50-200 turns of dense dialogue

rule.rationale: Unbounded history hits the model's input limit and either silently truncates (lossy) or 400s (broken). 10-turn cap is the empirical break-even between recall and bound.

rule.statement: Always pass `language="en"|"uk"|"de"|...` to Whisper. Forbid auto-detect in production loops; it adds ~200ms and occasionally misidentifies similar languages (uk/ru, sr/hr).

rationale.source: Whisper auto-detect benchmarks on similar languages

rule.rationale: Auto-detect costs latency on every turn (~200ms) and produces silent transcription errors on uk/ru pairs. Explicit language is a config item, not per-turn discovery.

rule.statement: Use `client.audio.speech.with_streaming_response.create(...)` and stream PCM chunks to the speaker / WebSocket. Forbid synchronous `stream_to_file` followed by playback.

rationale.source: First-byte-to-audio latency benchmarks: streaming ~100ms vs buffered ~400-600ms

rule.rationale: Streaming delivers first audio chunk while the rest is still synthesising. Buffered approach idles the user-perceived latency window.
